jobs.description: Job Description What is the Opportunity? The Actuarial Analyst will be responsible for the delivery of the RBC Insurance Economic and Regulatory Capital reporting including a number of the supporting components. This role also has accountabilities for building new Capital models, improving capital modeling methodologies, as well as maintaining and updating existing Capital models. The Actuarial Analyst will be mainly responsible for the delivery of the RBC Insurance Economic Capital reporting on quarterly basis. The role also assists in the regulatory capital reporting such as Life Insurance Capital Adequacy Test (LICAT) and Financial Condition Testing (FCT). What will you do? Support the quarter-end reporting for Economic Capital for RBC Insurance. This usually include model runs, model recalibrations, model improvements, data processing, and analysis of results. Support the identification, development, and implementation of enhancements to existing capital forecast methodologies and frameworks. Maintain Economic Capital model methodology documentations and model change procedures. Assist in LICAT forecasting, explaining quarterly movements and filing of LICAT results with OSFI before deadlines. Support ad-hoc projects and activities as required (mainly on IFRS 17 LICAT model change, other ad-hoc requests include methodology improvements, committee reporting, etc.) Assist with other RBC Insurance risk capital functionalities as needed, which may include Enterprise Wide Stress Testing (EWST) for insurance divisions, ORSA sensitivity test, and other ad-hoc stress testing. What do you need to succeed? Strong analytical and communication skills for carrying out accountabilities Strong technical skills in GGY AXIS, R, MS Excel and MS Access (SQL) 1 year of related actuarial work experience Solid understanding of actuarial principles, practices, and modeling logics for valuation and capital projections Excellent problem solving skills for identifying and recommending enhancements to existing frameworks and methodologies Knowledge of life insurance standards of practice, capital regime, reserving principles, insurance accounting, product design and administration, and pricing framework What’s in it for you? We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual. A comprehensive Total Rewards Program include competitive compensation and flexible benefits. Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities. Ability to make a difference and lasting impact. Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team. Opportunities to do challenging work. Opportunities to build close relationships with clients. You have the potential to earn more through RBC’s discretionary variable compensation program which gives you an opportunity to increase your total compensation, provided the business meets its performance targets and you meet your individual goals. RBC’s compensation philosophy and principles recognize the importance of a highly qualified global workforce and plays a critical role in attracting, engaging and retaining talent that: Drives RBC’s high-performance culture Enables collective achievement of our strategic goals Generates sustainable shareholder returns and above market shareholder value #LI – POST Job Skills Actuarial Modeling, Capital Adequacy, Economic Capital, Insurance Finance and Actuarial Concepts, Multi-Level Communication, Risk Analysis Management, Risk Assessments, Stress Testing Additional Job Details Address: MEADOWVALE BUSINESS PARK, 6880 FINANCIAL DR:MISSISSAUGA City: Mississauga Country: Canada Work hours/week: 37.5 Employment Type: Full time Platform: GROUP RISK MANAGEMENT Job Type: Regular Pay Type: Salaried Posted

jobs.description: Job Description What is the opportunity? RBC Dominion Securities is a relationship-focused investment and wealth management firm that consistently ranks #1 in Canada. We are proud of our collaborative, supportive culture that enables success at every level and equips us to help our clients achieve their financial goals. You will play a central role in scaling our family office services, working directly with clients and alongside our leadership team to support every part of the client experience, from first conversation through to long-term planning. The Wen Family Wealth Group is a family office wealth planning practice within RBC Dominion Securities, dedicated to serving high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth families and business owners. We operate as a private family office — guiding multi-generational wealth transfer, business succession planning, and integrated financial and wealth planning for the families we serve. Our philosophy is simple: we treat wealth challenges as human problems first. Behind every balance sheet is a family, a legacy, and a set of hopes for the next generation. We lead with warmth, build deep and lasting trust, and bring genuine care to every relationship. As we grow our family office services, we are looking for someone who shares that belief and wants to help us scale it. What will you do? Maintain existing client accounts and identify opportunities to contact clients. You will be responsible for managing timelines and key dates for accounts to help ensure clients feel supported by our team. Financial and wealth planning by supporting integrated financial and wealth planning for clients by preparing client reports, contributing to planning analysis and coordinating with our internal Wealth partners to book meetings and presentations. Tap into your superior problem resolution skills to provide proactive client service. Working collaboratively, you will at times liaise with various departments to provide smooth and timely solutions. Effectively manage incoming communications from clients, Advisors and other internal and external partners. Lead the client onboarding process with account openings and collaborate with team members for administrative coordination and ensuring a seamless process. Event & networking which entails planning and hosting client events and networking initiatives to deepen relationships with clients and COI’s. Draw on your social media skills to update the team’s communication channels, including websites, LinkedIn, Facebook, brochures, newsletters. Contributing to business development and continued growth of the practice. What do you need to succeed? Must-have Canadian Investment Regulatory Exam, CIRE. 2-3 years of financial planning or wealth management experience. Proficiency in financial planning with a strong understanding of finance and investment strategies. Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related field, or equivalent experience. Strong skills working with business applications/technology, including Microsoft Office Suite and Salesforce. High quality time management and organization skills. Demonstrated skills in providing world-class client service that will come across as professional, warm, and friendly to our clients. Exceptional verbal and written communication skills in English. Nice-to-have Certified Financial Planner (CFP) Knowledge of RBC Dominion Securities’ systems and procedures Mandarin and Cantonese language skills. What’s in it for you? We thrive on the challenge to be our best, to think progressively to keep growing, and to work together to deliver trusted advice that can help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other and collectively work hard to reach our potential, make a difference to our communities, and achieve succes

jobs.description: Job Description RBC Capital Markets is a part of the Royal Bank of Canada, one of the largest providers of financial services in the world. RBC ranks among the top five banks in North America by market capitalization and has one of the highest credit ratings of any financial institution globally. Our Global Investment Banking division provides underwriting of equity and debt capital as well as strategic M&A advisory services for leading corporations, institutions and governments throughout North America and around the world. RBC Capital Markets is Canada’s leading investment bank. We have built our successful investment banking business by focusing on our clients and the industry sectors in which they operate. With teams located in North America, Europe, Australia and Asia, we offer clients across the public and private sectors insightful financial advice, bring a competitive advantage on funding solutions, and assist in navigating complex investment decisions. What is the opportunity? RBC Capital Markets is seeking an Analyst to join its Indigenous Advisory and Finance Practice in Toronto. RBC Capital Markets’ Indigenous Advisory & Finance Practice aims to expand access to capital for new and existing Indigenous-owned major projects through advisory services, financing and capacity-building initiatives, while mitigating reputational and financial risks. The practice works in collaboration with RBC Origins as well as Commercial Banking and Wealth Management to support Indigenous communities and corporate clients across key sectors of the economy. The Indigenous Advisory and Finance Analyst will sit within RBC Capital Markets’ Sustainable Finance Group, alongside a centralized team of subject matter experts delivering integrated sustainability advice and innovative financial solutions to Capital Markets clients. The Sustainable Finance Group works in close collaboration with Corporate and Investment Banking coverage officers and Sales and Trading professionals to help clients navigate the transition to a lower-carbon, more resilient economy. This role offers exposure to both practices, providing a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of sustainable finance and Indigenous economic participation. What will you do? Evaluate and analyze the financial needs of clients, including developing financial models Support transaction structuring and execution efforts, with increasing levels of responsibility and accountability over time Financial and written analysis of companies and industries Contributing to the delivery of client meetings and presentations Research and analyze market trends, including developments in sustainable finance, energy transition, and Indigenous economic participation Contribute to the execution of equity and debt financings, mergers and acquisitions, and financial advisory assignments Assist in the development of thought leadership, events, and client-facing materials on sustainable finance and Indigenous finance topics What do you need to succeed? Undergraduate degree program in Finance, Accounting, Engineering or a quantitative field from a leading university Strong quantitative skills focused on financial analysis, accounting and financial theory Strong verbal and written communication skills A high level of attention to detail Ability to manage multiple competing priorities and thrive in a fast-paced and challenging environment Value and possess strong competency in the following behaviours: Collaboration, Relationship Building, Communication, Integrity and Operating with Diverse and Inclusive Mindset The ability to perform effectively in a team environment and excel under pressure amid demanding deadlines Well-rounded set of interests and extra-curricular activities beyond academics Desire to develop a leadership skillset and make meaningful contributions in a fast-paced, team environment CFA, CPA or other relevant professional designations are considered an asset, but not a requirement 1-2 years

jobs.description: At Fiera Capital We invest in creating a culture of purpose that makes our people feel valued, cared for, seen, and heard. Working closely with Investment Counsellors, you will assist with administrative and project tasks related to client servicing and portfolio management, while gaining valuable exposure to the broader investment management process. This is a learning-focused position, ideal for someone curious about financial services and eager to explore the many career paths within the industry. Open to Indigenous students or recent graduates from any educational background. Proficiency in English (spoken and written) required. This is 4-month internship, with the possibility of extension. jobs.description: We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients. At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute. To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com What you’ll be doing As a member of the Personal and Business Banking team, you’ll work in a fast-paced Banking Centre where you’ll make a meaningful difference in our clients' lives. As an Associate Financial Advisor, you’ll form a deep understanding of your personal and small business clients’ needs as you recommend simple, personalized solutions that will help them achieve their financial goals. You’ll manage, either individually or as part of a team, a portfolio of mortgage clients where the focus is on deepening the client relationship. You’re flexible to work our banking centre hours which may include evenings and weekends. To help deliver a great client experience, you’re flexible to work at multiple banking centres within a reasonable travel distance.  At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. To successfully perform the work, you’ll be on-site full-time. How you’ll succeed Client engagement - Meet with clients to understand their personal and business priorities, advise them on solutions, and provide a forward-looking financial plan. Relationship building - Engage in marketing and outreach activities to show clients you value them and their communities. Grow your network, deepen existing relationships, and work as one team to ensure clients are connected to the right people and opportunities. Business development - Create relationships by networking with clients’ families and friends to uncover opportunities and refer to appropriate partners. Call both existing and potential clients to identify opportunities for future growth and revenue. Engage in community and banking centre events such as business development events, client acknowledgement programs and client appreciation day to create and enhance CIBC presence and gain market share. Leveraging technology - Become a technology expert. Share your knowledge by introducing clients to our mobile banking applications, helping them to better manage their banking needs. Who you are You put our clients first. You engage with purpose to find the right solutions. You go the extra mile, because it’s the right thing to do. You’re goal oriented. You’re motivated by accomplishing your goals and delivering your best to make a difference. You know that details matter. You notice things that others don't. Your critical thinking skills help to inform your decision making. You’re passionate about people. You find meaning in relationships and surround yourself with a diverse network of partners. You connect with others through respect and authenticity. You love to learn. You’re passionate about growing your knowledge. You have a strong sense of curiosity. You can demonstrate 2 – 3 years experience in providing financial advice to clients and achieving sales results through advice-based conversations. You’re a certified professional. You have current accreditation and good standing in Mutual Funds License (Canadian Securities Course or Investment Funds in Canada). It’s an asset if you have current accreditation and good standing in CFSA (Certificate of Financial Services Advice). You’re a certified professional (Ontario). jobs.description: Job Description WHAT IS THE OPPORTUNITY? jobs.description: At Fiera Capital We invest in creating a culture of purpose that makes our people feel valued, cared for, seen, and heard. Our approach to employee experience is tailored to your needs and ambitions: Your Inclusive Experience: We are committed to cultivating an inclusive, safe, and trusting work environment. Your Growth & Empowerment: We have ambitious growth goals for our firm, which makes us a great place to advance your career. Your Rewards & Recognition: We deeply value our people and their contributions and that’s reflected in our competitive compensation and benefits packages and our collaborative culture. Your Wellness Your Way: We strive to create a healthy work environment and we offer programs designed to support our employees’ wellbeing. What we are looking for: Positioned within the Public Markets division under Global Trading, our Implementation and Portfolio Rebalancing (IPR Private Wealth) team is pivotal to Fiera Capital's Private Wealth trading operations. As a Junior Analyst, you will play a crucial role in the implementation and rebalancing of client portfolios, making a direct impact on their financial outcomes. We are looking for proactive individuals who are ready to take the lead on initiatives, drive projects forward, and advocate for ongoing process enhancements within our structured team environment. Your responsibilities: As a Junior Analyst, your core responsibilities will include: Order Generation: Efficiently rebalance Private Wealth client portfolios according to Investment Policy Statements (IPS) and tactical targets, manage trading operations including regular deposits, withdrawals, and fee adjustments, and align trading with private market fund capital calls and redemptions. Model Implementation: Execute the implementation of segregated models across equities and bonds, ensuring alignment with client goals and market conditions. Process Innovation: Take an active role in the design and enhancement of operational processes, pushing for automation and efficiency improvements in collaboration with stakeholders from across the firm. Global Transformation Support: Contribute to the firm’s global transformation efforts by sharing best practices and insights from the team’s operations. Client and Team Support: Assist Investment Counsellors and Management with detailed investment policy drafting and provide critical business information to support decision-making processes. Must have requirements to be successful in this role: Educational Background: A Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Mathematics, Actuarial Sciences, or another quantitative field. Advanced Qualifications: A Master’s degree in Finance, Financial Engineering, or Economics is highly desirable. Additional qualifications such as a CFA or CAIA designation, or risk management certifications like PRM or FRM are advantageous. Relevant Experience: 1-3 years in investment management, client servicing, or operational roles within the financial services industry. Performance Under Deadlines: Order generation runs on firm, often same-day timelines, with requests that can carry short turnarounds. This is a deadline-driven environment, and we look for candidates who stay organized, composed, and accurate under time pressure — and who genuinely thrive when the pace picks up. Technical Skills: Proficiency in analytical and programming tools such as SQL, Python, Power BI, VBA for Excel, or comparable technologies, with a strong track record of applying these tools in handling complex data structures. AI Fluency: Genuine fluency with AI and large language models (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini) — and the coding harnesses built on them (e.g., Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot) — as everyday working tools, applied to real analytical and productivity challenges. We place strong value on candidates who actively follow the rapid pace of model releases and experiment independently to find where these tools can sharpen quantitative work. jobs.description: Location Brookfield Place - 181 Bay Street Business - Energy Brookfield Energy operates one of the world’s largest publicly traded platforms for sustainable energy. Our energy portfolio consists of hydroelectric, wind, utility-scale solar, distributed generation and storage facilities in North America, South America, Europe and Asia. Our operating capacity totals over 35,000 megawatts and our development pipeline stands at approximately 200,000 megawatts. Our portfolio of sustainable solutions assets includes our investments in Westinghouse (a leading global nuclear services business) and a utility and independent power producer with operations in the Caribbean and Latin America, as well as both operating assets and a development pipeline of carbon capture and storage capacity, agricultural renewable natural gas and materials recycling. Brookfield Energy is the flagship listed energy company of Brookfield Asset Management, a leading global alternative asset manager with over $1 trillion of assets under management. To learn more about the Brookfield Energy group, visit https://www.brookfield.com/about-us/capabilities/energy. Brookfield Culture Brookfield has a unique and dynamic culture. We seek team members who have a long-term focus and whose values align with our Attributes of a Brookfield Leader: Entrepreneurial, Collaborative and Disciplined. Brookfield is committed to the development of our people through challenging work assignments and exposure to diverse businesses. Job Description Reporting to the Managing Director, Investments, Brookfield is seeking an Associate to join the Brookfield Energy investments team in Toronto. Brookfield Energy invests in and operates one of the world’s largest renewable power and sustainable solutions platforms, with assets across hydro, wind, solar, storage, nuclear services, carbon capture, renewable natural gas, recycling and other transition-related businesses. The team is focused on investing behind long-term structural trends, including growing demand for power, electrification, energy security, industrial decarbonization and the build-out of digital infrastructure. The Associate will support the evaluation and execution of investments across renewable power, transition infrastructure and hard-to-abate sectors, focused on North America, including opportunities for the Brookfield Global Transition Fund (BGTF) and other Brookfield strategies. Responsibilities Build financial models, valuation analyses and return assessments for new investment opportunities. Conduct company, sector and market research to support investment theses and diligence. Assess key value drivers, risks, financing structures and potential transaction outcomes. Support the sourcing, evaluation and execution of investments across renewable power, infrastructure, sustainable solutions, and business transformation and partnership opportunities. Evaluate decarbonization impact in line with Brookfield’s transition investment framework. Work with Brookfield operating teams to support commercial, operational and technical diligence. Prepare investment committee materials and transaction documentation. Support workstreams related to post-investment development opportunities, emissions reduction, operational improvement and value creation. Contribute to a disciplined, collaborative and performance-oriented team culture. Key Qualifications 1-3 years of relevant experience in investment banking or a related field. Strong financial modeling, valuation and analytical skills. Strong communication skills and ability to independently manage multiple priorities. Sound commercial judgment and attention to detail. Interest in energy, infrastructure, industrials, and the energy transition; experience in energy, power, infrastructure, or industrials considered an asset. Conduct market research, comparable project analysis, demographic review, rental analysis, competitive assessments, and trend monitoring to inform development decisions and assess factors impacting current or future projects. Assist with land acquisition opportunities, including due diligence, financial analysis, project proformas, budgets, and investment materials. Help review and coordinate consultant agreements, construction-related agreements, municipal documentation, and other project materials, in partnership with internal stakeholders and legal counsel. Work with the Construction team during project execution to monitor progress, address coordination items, and support alignment between approved drawings, site conditions, schedule, budget, and delivery objectives. Maintain organized project records, files, correspondence, approvals documentation, and reporting materials. Provide additional support across property management, asset management, development administration, and broader team priorities as required. jobs.description: ABOUT US We’re one of Canada’s largest pension investors, with CAD$299.7 billion of net assets as of March 31, 2025. We invest funds for the pension plans of the federal public service, the Canadian Forces, the Royal Canadian Mounted Police and the Reserve Force. Headquartered in Ottawa, PSP Investments has its principal business office in Montréal and offices in New York, London and Hong Kong. Capturing and leading complex global investments requires us to work as one to seize valuable opportunities, in close collaboration with some of the world’s top companies. At PSP Investments, you’ll join a team of motivated and engaged professionals, dedicated to propelling our organization further than ever before. ABOUT THE TEAM The Portfolio Management team supports the Real Estate group in ensuring timely access to accurate and relevant data to support investment decisions and provide governance of business and operational processes. This includes reporting production and oversight of the inventory of systems, documents and databases. ABOUT YOUR ROLE As an Intern, Portfolio Management, Real Estate Investments, you’ll: Enter, update and reconcile data in different systems and databases Prepare and clean data for system implementations Participate in the test execution for new system implementations Apply data governance processes and controls Assist in the production of various reports and dashboards Support team with various operational activities Support the Portfolio Management team for various requests WHAT YOU’LL NEED Currently enrolled in a university degree in Finance, Business and IT or a related discipline High level of proficiency in Excel Intermediate knowledge of SQL, an asset Someone who enjoys learning new things and a true curiosity for the world around them A proactive and entrepreneurial attitude Proven ability to work in a team setting and to easily adapt to change Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail Must return to studies after the internship or the internship must be part of your curriculum Candidates must have the right to work in Canada* for the period specified to be considered for this position. No support to obtain the right to work will be provided *Options include Canadian citizenship, permanent residency or a valid open work permit Good communications skills in English and French (or willingness to learn) (regular interactions in English with investment partners based outside of Quebec Example: peers, bankers, brokerage firms, etc. and PSP employees in our offices in Hong Kong, London and New York, and interactions in French with employees in our local offices in Montreal and Ottawa) At PSP Investments, we aim to provide a workplace where everyone feels valued, safe, respected and empowered to grow. They work to identify vulnerabilities, develop secure systems, and provide proactive solutions to safeguard sensitive data. As a cybersecurity generalist at PwC, you will focus on providing comprehensive security solutions and experience across various domains, maintaining the protection of client systems and data. You will apply a broad understanding of cybersecurity principles and practices to address diverse security challenges effectively. The incumbent will develop and maintain working relationships with line of business (LOB) and audit partners across the organization including Group Risk Management, Personal & Commercial Banking, Capital Markets, Finance, Wealth Management Internal/External Audit, SOX Office, and other enterprise functions. What will you do? Support the Standardized Approach to Key Controls (SAKC) control identification methodology and ensure consistent application across all Critical Risk and Finance Reports and key business processes. Gain and apply an in-depth understanding of key control groups (e.g. data input, data transfer, business rule, data quality, ITGC) and how each control type would most effectively apply in a given data flow or system scenario. Evaluate any unmitigated risks and control weaknesses, and share recommendations for new or enhanced controls consistent with the Enterprise Data Policy. Work with report and business process owners and delegates to understand exception scenarios, based on materiality thresholds, where critical data element instances or individuals systems might be immaterial. Collaborate with lines of business (LOB) functions in the bank, other Data Management Offices (DMOs), SOX Office, and Audit to facilitate data control enhancement and/or implementation where needed. Participate in some reviews of the CDE Memos, data lineage and control information for Finance, Risk and other critical reports, key business processes and data domains. Provide support by tracking status of team objectives by maintaining and updating the team’s JIRA board on SAKC progress. What do you need to succeed? 1-2+ years of experience in risk management, data management and business analysis. Some experience with credit risk data, data quality principles, data control design, self-assessment of existing processes and systems. Undergraduate degree in a numerical discipline, business administration or a combination. Good communications skills, verbal and written. Some experience with business and/or technical documentation and the assessment of existing processes and systems. Nice-to-have: Self-starter, highly motivated, having a strong drive to achieve tangible results. Ability to manage multiple competing priorities in times of stress. Ability to analyze and comprehend complex data related issues. Familiarity with financial service regulatory requirements and guidelines (e.g. BCBS239, IFRS 9, CECL) What’s in it for you? We thrive on the challenge to be our best, thinking progressively to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual. A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities Ability to make a difference and lasting impact Work in an agile, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team The opportunity to interface with executives from many different parts of the organization Job Skills Control Assessment, Critical Thinking, Data Controls, Detail-Oriented, Group Problem Solving, ITGC, Key Control, Office Administration, Risk Management, Standard Operating Procedure (SOP), Teamwork Additional Job Details Address: 175 WESTERN PKY:BEDFORD City: Bedford Country: Canada Work hours/week: 37.5 Employment Type: Full time Platform: CHIEF LEGAL

Maintains end-to-end documentation of workflows and processes to support auditability, traceability, and repeatability. Data, Inventory & Reporting Support Produces and manages structured datasets to support risk oversight, monitoring, and reporting activities. Supports data quality management, including validation, standardization, and remediation of data gaps. Assists in the development of reporting outputs, dashboards, and metrics, ensuring consistency with data governance standards. Analyzes data to identify trends, process gaps, and potential risk impacts related to AI use cases and other risk initiatives. AI Risk & Oversight Support Supports execution of AI risk officer activities, including intake review, portfolio monitoring, and use case assessments, as assigned. Performs initial analysis of new initiatives or workflows to identify potential risk considerations, escalating to senior team members as appropriate. Assists with the interpretation of regulatory requirements and internal policies, and their application to governance frameworks and processes. Supports execution of second line of defense (2LOD) oversight activities, ensuring alignment with established risk frameworks and standards. Governance, Change & Stakeholder Support Supports the execution of governance and oversight processes, including reporting, attestations, and tracking of action items. Assists with governance forums and working groups, including coordination of materials, documentation, and follow-ups. Contributes to change management activities, primarily focused on execution, implementation, and sustainment. Supports the development of training materials, user guidance, and communication artifacts to improve adoption and consistency. Works with stakeholders to improve transparency, consistency, and usability of processes, metrics, and reporting outputs. General Risk & Operational Support Administers and maintains risk program activities in accordance with policies, standards, and established procedures. Monitors and tracks workflow performance, identifying and addressing operational issues. Maintains tools, templates, and supporting artifacts used across risk programs (e.g., RCSA, regulatory tracking, inventory management), as required. Develops and maintains working knowledge of AI risk, operational risk, and relevant regulatory expectations. Builds effective working relationships with internal stakeholders and corporate support areas. Exercises judgment to identify and resolve problems within defined guidelines; escalates complex or ambiguous issues. Works independently on assigned tasks, including those involving non-routine or evolving requirements. Supports additional AI risk and adjacent risk activities as required, based on evolving priorities and regulatory expectations. Salary: $61,600.00

jobs.description: ENGLISH FOLLOW La personne que nous recherchons Le rôle du middle office au sein de l’organisation consiste à gérer les contrôles, les exécutions et les décisions critiques et urgentes au sein des opérations d’investissement. Les équipes des opérations de gestion sont responsables de la gestion des positions, de la confirmation et des règlements des opérations, du traitement des actions d’entreprise, de la gestion des garanties, de la trésorerie et du rapprochement des positions De nombreuses équipes sont directement en contact avec les clients et agissent en leur nom tout en interagissant avec des parties internes et externes. L’importance de ce rôle pour nous Les opérations de produits dérivés gèrent le traitement et le règlement des transactions dérivées au nom du client et des clients associés. Ce dont vous serez responsable Gérer les règlements commerciaux, le rapprochement et la confirmation des produits dérivés tels que les échanges financiers, les contrats à terme et les options. Examiner l’entente de l’ISDA et de la CSA pour les contrats bilatéraux. Confirmer les nouvelles opérations et les paiements initiaux et à terme avec les contreparties. Surveiller Markitserv, Tradeserv et Tradeweb pour assurer des confirmations en temps opportun. Assurer la surveillance du DTCC pour les incidents de crédit et le traitement de ces incidents. Traiter les rajustements du taux d’intérêt nominal de gré à gré et les swaps de défaillance de crédit. Effectuer les rapprochements de gré à gré par l’entremise de Trioptima. Résoudre les coupures de trésorerie et gérer les requêtes. Assurer l’ensemble de la gestion post-négociation et du cycle de vie de tous les produits dérivés de gré à gré et négociés en bourse qui sont compensés et négociés bilatéralement. Assurer la confirmation / l’affirmation des opérations et les soumettre à la compensation. Rapprocher les dossiers internes avec les contreparties et les chambres de compensation, résoudre les ruptures et les écarts. Traiter les événements du cycle de vie commercial tels que les réinitialisations, les expirations, l’exercice et les transferts. Assurer la confirmation / l’affirmation des opérations, notamment surveiller le statut de compensation avant et après la gestion de la marge initiale. Gérer les garanties, notamment la préparation des estimations et des rapprochements des différends. Assurer la surveillance et la notification concernant les événements de revenus et d’entreprise. Gérer les pénuries de garanties et le processus de substitution. Effectuer le rapprochement et le traitement des marges initiales et de variation des produits dérivés négociés en bourse et de compensation de gré à gré. Effectuer le rapprochement des transactions et des positions des contrats à terme avec le courtier en compensation pour s’assurer que les livres du courtier exécutant et du courtier en compensation sont conformes aux livres SS. Formation et qualifications privilégiées Baccalauréat/maîtrise en commerce/économie avec spécialisation en administration des affaires, en comptabilité, en finances ou l’équivalent. Être à l’aise avec l’ambiguïté et avoir la capacité de bien travailler avec les équipes, dans plusieurs fonctions et zones géographiques. Capacité de travailler dans un environnement collaboratif pour obtenir des résultats. Est ouvert à la possibilité de travailler selon l’horaire de travail de l’Amérique du Nord. Solides aptitudes essentielles en anglais oral et écrit pour servir les clients internationaux. Exigences supplémentaires Mettre l’accent sur le client : offrir un excellent service et bâtir des partenariats stratégiques fondés sur la connaissance des besoins et le dépassement des attentes mutuelles. Gérer la mise en œuvre et assurer une communication efficace : offrir une prestation exemplaire des produits du travail grâce à des plans efficaces, des priorités claires et des méthodes avancées de gestion des processus, pour faire en sorte que toutes les ressources fonctionnent à

Make good use of technology to connect with clients both virtually and face to face Implement effective contact and relationship building strategies, that accelerate new client acquisition and retention in your local community Collaborate with market-leading professionals in financial planning, retirement planning, mortgages, and business banking experts to ensure clients receive customized and relevant expert advice What do you need to succeed? BMO Capital Markets is a member of BMO Financial Group (NYSE, TSX: BMO), one of the largest diversified financial services providers in North America with US$1.0 trillion total assets and approximately 54,000 employees as of January 31, 2025. About the Role We are seeking a talented and experienced Data Scientist to join our Data Cognition Team at BMO Capital Markets. As a Data Scientist, you will be responsible for developing and implementing advanced analytics and machine learning models to support algorithmic trading strategies, and drive business decisions in the front office, Investment Banking, and Corporate Banking. You will collaborate closely with business teams and other stakeholders to understand their data needs and provide insights that improve trading performance and profitability. Our Team The Data Cognition Team (DCT) at BMO Capital Markets delivers a scalable, customizable, and sustainable suite of AI-enabled products for multiple business units. Leveraging cutting-edge data and analytics technologies, we solve complex business challenges and drive strategic transformation across Investment Banking, Global Markets, and other divisions. Key Responsibilities · Develop and implement advanced analytics and machine learning models using techniques such as Generative AI, Deep Learning, and Reinforcement Learning. · Build predictive models for algorithmic trading across asset classes including Equities, Fixed Income, Commodities, and Foreign Exchange. · Collaborate with trading desks, investment banking teams, and corporate banking stakeholders to identify opportunities and optimize strategies. · Research and experiment with new machine learning techniques, including time series analysis, to improve trading performance and profitability. · Design and maintain reproducible machine learning pipelines for production environments. · Analyze large datasets to extract insights and support strategic decision-making. · Communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders and present findings to senior management. · Visualize complex data clearly and concisely for business consumption. Qualifications · Ph.D. in Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, or a related quantitative field (focus on finance, economics, or operations research preferred). · Minimum 3 years of experience in data science or machine learning; experience in capital markets is a strong asset. · Proficiency in statistical modeling, time series analysis, and machine learning frameworks. · Strong programming skills (Python preferred) and experience with cloud technologies. · Familiarity with financial tools such as Bloomberg, FactSet, and Thomson Reuters is a plus. · Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to formulate hypotheses and learn from data. · Strong communication skills for effective collaboration with technical and business stakeholders. · Team-oriented, adaptable, and detail-focused with a results-driven mindset. Nice to Have · Experience with Generative AI and advanced ML techniques. · Knowledge of investment banking concepts, trading strategies, and financial data formats. · Familiarity with MLOps tools and practices for model deployment and monitoring. What We Offer Join our dynamic team at BMO Capital Markets and contribute to solving complex challenges in investment banking and global markets using innovative technologies. This information is relied upon by the Investments, Accounting, Risk, and Legal teams to support reporting, compliance, and decision-making. The analyst collaborates with cross-functional teams to resolve discrepancies, strengthen reporting capabilities, and enhance data management workflows. Responsibilities Oversee the execution and maintenance of business and control processes that ensure security master records are properly established, maintained, and delivered to relevant stakeholders in a complete, reliable, and timely manner. Supports the identification and resolution of business process gaps that hinder the Operation’s team from delivering reliable daily security master reference data. Contributes to cross-functional and organization-wide initiatives, such as Aladdin MVP, focused on enhancing the overall quality of security data. Partners with internal teams and external auditors to enhance the design and effectiveness of existing control frameworks, and to recommend and implement additional controls where necessary. Experience with order management systems (e.g., Aladdin), data vendors (e.g., Bloomberg, Refinitiv), reconciliation tools (e.g., DUCO), and accounting systems (e.g., Clearwater). Knowledge of private assets, fixed income, and structured products is preferred. Experienced in handling and organizing large volumes of data. Proficient in Microsoft Excel, including Macros, to analyze and manipulate data efficiently. Understands regulatory-driven asset classification and its impact on rating and reporting processes Strong communication and interpersonal skills, including verbal, written, and presentation abilities, with a proven ability to work effectively with colleagues, clients, and external partners. Detail-oriented and analytical, skilled at reviewing data, solving practical problems, and drawing accurate conclusions. Highly organized and adaptable, able to manage multiple priorities, meet deadlines, and perform under pressure in a fast-paced environment. Technical proficiency, including Microsoft Office, SQL, and the preparation of reports, business correspondence, and procedural documentation. jobs.description: We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients. At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute. To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com What You'll Be Doing As an Analyst you will be expected to provide a broad range of analytical and research support to all professionals within the Financial Institutions & Public Sector Lending group of CIBC Corporate Banking. The Analyst will be involved with a variety of client transactions that expose them to various aspects of loan origination, deal structuring and execution. The Analyst will work with a number of senior partners internally to support a wide range of large corporate client relationships. Responsibilities include financial statement and credit analysis, financial modeling, due diligence, industry research and assisting in the preparation of credit narratives, presentation materials and client pitch books. The role of an Analyst is challenging and fast-paced. It requires dedication, passion for the industry and a very solid work ethic. At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. To successfully perform the work, you’ll be on-site full-time. How You'll Succeed Analysis - Responsible for conducting in-depth company, industry and portfolio analysis to support the credit relationships for a portfolio of clients including but not limited to pension funds, banks, insurers, asset managers, mortgage originators, auto finance companies, and public sector entities. Lending Recommendations - Conduct sound, timely and well-communicated recommendations for credit facilities supported by thorough analysis and well-executed interpretation of relevant financial, industry, business and market information. Evaluation - Run financial models and analysis, mainly focused on credit underwriting, lending returns, and risk rating evaluation. jobs.role: Investment Associate, Private Capital - Financial Services, Services, or Technology (Multiple Opportunities)
jobs.notes: Org-scan (workday careers page, CAASA org list). Posted 6 Days Ago.
jobs.description: The opportunity We are seeking 4 new Associates to join our Private Capital (“PC”) team across multiple sector pods. Based in Toronto, you will be an integral part of a team focused on direct investments in North America. You will play a key role in evaluating potential investment opportunities, executing transactions, conducting sector research including transaction pipeline building, and supporting portfolio company management. Who you'll work with You will work with a diverse team of professionals based in Toronto, focused on evaluating new opportunities and managing investments. In your role, you will engage with management teams of portfolio companies, fund partners, investment bankers, board directors, and advisors. You will be aligned to one of three sector pods: Financial Services: The Financial Services team invests across sub-sectors including insurance, wealth & asset management, lending, professional services, and cards & payments, with a particular need to understand the regulatory, AI, data and digitization themes reshaping the sector. You will work with sector specialists and portfolio company management to evaluate new investments, support value creation initiatives, and develop sub-sector views across priority areas. Services: A team focused on commercial & residential, industrial & infrastructure, and durable customer services. You will work alongside experienced deal professionals on platform and add-on acquisitions, engaging directly with management teams to drive operational improvements and growth. Technology: A team focused on investments in durable software and technology-enabled services businesses. Sub-sectors of focus include vertical and horizontal software (including healthcare IT, financial services, Office of the CFO, supply chain, and GRC), cybersecurity software and services, managed service providers (MSPs), and digital transformation. You will work alongside experienced deal professionals on platform and add-on investments, engaging with management teams to support growth, operational improvement, and technology-enabled value creation. What you'll do You will evaluate new investments and determine whether the company represents an attractive investment opportunity. The analysis of investments includes conducting industry and company research, managing third-party advisors, building sophisticated financial models, arranging debt financing, reviewing legal documents, reviewing and analyzing due diligence materials, and preparing internal support materials and written recommendations to PC’s Investment Committee. Once a transaction has been completed, you will remain involved with the business, potentially attending board meetings as an observer, and will work with the management team and the board of directors to implement value-enhancing initiatives. In addition, you will work directly with management on various initiatives as required, including with respect to mergers and acquisitions, debt refinancing, budgeting and other strategic projects. You will help develop and refresh sub-sector investment theses through deep dives, market mapping, primary research, target screening, valuation benchmarking, and identification of key diligence themes. Activities will include conducting desk research, working with bankers and consultants, and attending conferences. There is also the expectation that you assist in building and maintaining relationships with fund partners. What you'll need Minimum 2 years related work experience (such as private equity, investment banking, M&A advisory, management consulting, or corporate development) An undergraduate or graduate degree from a university with a focus on business / finance / accounting / investment analysis, or equivalent experience Experience in developing financial models (LBO, DCF, etc.) from the ground up to aid in investment valuation, transaction structuring, and financing decisions. Experience evaluating companies, transactions, or investment theme

jobs.description: Job Description What is the opportunity? As the Associate, Trade Finance Solutions, you will support the delivery and growth of Trade Finance and Working Capital solutions within RBC Commercial Banking. You will work closely with Trade Finance Sales Managers, Relationship Managers, Credit, Operations, Legal and Compliance partners to support client deal execution and deliver a high-quality client experience. This role provides exposure to a broad range of trade finance products, client segments, and internal stakeholders, while serving as a strong development platform for future advancement within Trade Finance or Commercial Banking. You will contribute analytical, administrative, and deal execution support to ensure transactions are completed accurately, efficiency, and in accordance with RBC’s risk and compliance standards. What will you do? Provide sales and execution support to Trade Finance sales leads and Relationship Managers by preparing pitch materials, product overviews, and client-specific trade finance solutions Assist in coordinating client meetings, preparing meeting materials, capturing notes, and following up on actions Conduct working capital analysis and profitability calculations to support client decisions and deal structuring Support end-to-end execution of trade finance transactions, including client onboarding, documentation, and coordination with internal partners Review, compile, and track documentation requirements for trade transactions Support preparation of credit submissions and assist with internal approval processes Update systems with opportunity details, client engagement notes, and pipeline activity Track deal milestones and support execution workflows to ensure timely completion and client satisfaction Collaborate with Credit, Product, Legal, Operations and Compliance teams to support transaction execution Maintain accurate records and documentation to support audit readiness and regulatory requirements Actively build foundational trade finance knowledge to contribute to internal and client discussions on product use cases What do you need to succeed? Must-have: Undergraduate degree in business, finance, economics or a related field 2-4+ years of experience in client facing roles preferably in commercial or corporate banking Foundational understanding of financial products, credit principles, and commercial banking concepts Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously Strong organizational, communication and stakeholder coordination skills Analytical mindset with the ability to review documentation and identify issues or gaps Proactive, curious, and client-focused approach to work Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment Nice to have: Exposure to Trade Finance, Working Capital, or Commercial Banking Trade finance certification, CFA, CPA or other relevant professional designation Internship or co-op experience in financial services or banking What’s in it for you? We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual. Ability to make a difference and lasting impact Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team Opportunities to do challenging work Job Skills Commercial Banking, Commercial Banking Operations, Trade Finance Operations, Trade Finance Products Additional Job Details Address: VANCOUVER MAIN BRANCH (B), 1055 GEORGIA ST W:VANCOUVER City: Vancouver Country: Canada Work hours/week: 37.5 Employment Type: Full time Platform: PERSONAL & COMMERCIAL BANKING Job Type: Regular Pay Type: Salaried Posted Date: 2026-06-05 Application Deadline: 2026-06-20 Note: Applications will be accepted until 11:59 PM on the day prior to the application deadline

jobs.role: Adjoint ou adjointe, Expansion des affaires, Investly - Montréal / Investly Business Development Associate - Montreal
jobs.notes: Org-scan (workday careers page, CAASA org list). Posted Today.
jobs.description: Job Description À noter : Quiconque pose sa candidature doit disposer d’une autorisation de travail au Canada. Vous travaillerez selon un horaire hybride dans le cadre de la formule de travail dynamique de Fidelity. Il s’agit d’un poste permanent à temps plein. Le titulaire bilingue du poste est tenu de communiquer directement avec nos clients et nos collègues. Les clients doivent être servis dans la langue officielle de leur choix (français ou anglais). Qui nous sommes Chez Fidelity, nous aidons les investisseurs canadiens à prendre leur avenir financier en main depuis plus de 35 ans. Nous offrons aux particuliers et aux institutions une gamme de portefeuilles et de services de placement fiables, et nous cherchons constamment de nouvelles façons de mieux servir notre clientèle. Alors que nous poursuivons notre expansion en tant que société privée, nous sommes résolument portés par l’innovation dans tous nos domaines d’activité. Travailler chez nous veut dire faire partie d’un groupe diversifié de personnes motivées qui font une réelle différence dans la vie de notre clientèle et des collectivités chaque jour. Vous aurez de nombreuses occasions de vous épanouir et de faire progresser votre carrière dans un milieu inclusif où l’on reconnaît votre valeur et vous soutient, une place qui vous encourage à donner le meilleur de vous-même, tant sur le plan personnel que professionnel. Investly est l’application d’investissement de Fidelity Canada qui a été mise en ligne sur les magasins d’applications en 2024. Nous nous concentrons actuellement à offrir des solutions d’investissement guidé aux Canadiens et aux Canadiennes, ce qui signifie que nous sélectionnons, achetons et gérons les investissements à leur place pour qu’ils puissent consacrer leur temps à autre chose. Autrement dit, nous leur recommandons un portefeuille conçu par des experts qui correspond à leurs objectifs. Votre contribution L’adjoint ou l’adjointe, Expansion des affaires d’Investly, veillera à ce que l’acquisition d’utilisateurs et utilisatrices, d’investisseurs et investisseuses et de comptes financés soit aussi efficace et rentable que possible. La personne titulaire de ce poste en apprendra davantage sur le public investisseur canadien, cernera ses besoins et lui proposera des solutions qui peuvent l’aider à atteindre ses objectifs financiers. Dans le cadre de ses fonctions, cette personne sera également une source de renseignements et de données importantes pour guider le lancement de nouveaux produits. Principales responsabilités : 1) Parrainage et développement de partenariats Repérer les occasions de parrainage avec des organismes, des clubs et des événements locaux qui correspondent aux objectifs d’affaires, notamment l’ouverture de comptes financés et l’acquisition d’actifs. Élaborer une feuille de route des initiatives régionales pour le développement continu des affaires et la promotion d’Investly auprès du public afin d’atteindre les cibles de vente. Collaborer avec le Service de marketing, le Service de la conformité et le Service de la conformité des courtiers pour veiller à ce que les événements soient conformes à nos lignes directrices. 2) Stratégie régionale et transmission de l’information Recueillir et analyser les tendances du marché régional et les données des utilisateurs et utilisatrices pour éclairer la stratégie. Obtenir et évaluer les commentaires qualitatifs de la clientèle actuelle et potentielle en les entrant dans le système de gestion des relations avec la clientèle ou un système équivalent. Utiliser des outils d’intelligence artificielle (IA) internes approuvés, comme Co-Pilot, afin de mieux comprendre les tactiques de vente et orienter la stratégie. Collaborer avec le Service de la croissance, le Service des finances et le Service de l’expérience numérique de la clientèle. Valider et communiquer des rapports réguliers sur les activités, les résultats et les occasions. Veiller à l’exactitude des dossiers dans le système de gestion

jobs.notes: Org-scan (workday careers page, CAASA org list). Posted 6 Days Ago.
jobs.description: Application Deadline: Address: 100 King Street West Job Family Group: Capital Mrkts Sales & Service BMO Capital Markets is a leading, full-service financial services provider. We offer corporate and investment banking, treasury management, as well as research and advisory services to clients around the world. #bmocapitalmarkets About the Role This AI Product Ownership and Enablement role is a cross-functional position within BMO Capital Markets’ Data Cognition Team (DCT), suited for an early-career to mid-level professional who can work across business, technology, and vendor teams while helping scale adoption of enterprise AI tools across Global Markets (GM) and Investment & Corporate Banking (I&CB). The role spans Microsoft Copilot and Copilot Studio, while also supporting the onboarding and integration of emerging AI platforms such as Azure AI Studio, Anthropic Claude, and other enterprise AI solutions. You will help manage day-to-day product ownership, coordinate user enablement, and support rollout efforts that improve workflow quality, user experience, and productivity. Working closely with business stakeholders, enterprise technology teams, and external partners, you will play an important role in helping scale AI from pilot to production across Capital Markets. Our Team The Data Cognition Team (DCT) is BMO Capital Markets’ AI engineering and enablement group, with nearly a decade of experience deploying AI solutions in complex and regulated financial environments. DCT combines deep technical expertise, product thinking, and business acumen to turn emerging AI capabilities into practical, scalable tools that deliver measurable value. We work closely with business leaders, technologists, and external partners to ensure AI solutions move beyond experimentation and become embedded in front-office workflows. Key Responsibilities Copilot Product Ownership: Coordinate day-to-day product ownership activities for Microsoft 365 Copilot across Capital Markets to support adoption, stability, and continuous improvement. Serve as a liaison across business users, enterprise technology teams, and external partners to help prioritize features and enhancements. User Enablement and Support: Manage intake, triage, and resolution of non-technical user issues to improve responsiveness and user confidence. Partner with cross-functional teams to deliver a consistent, high-quality user experience across AI tools and support channels. Training, Adoption, and Change Management: Develop and deliver training materials that help Capital Markets users adopt AI tools more effectively in their day-to-day workflows. Drive user engagement through regular stakeholder interaction, feedback collection, and targeted enablement efforts. Content and Prompt Optimization: Maintain and improve the prompt library to better support Capital Markets workflows and common user needs. Use user feedback and usage patterns to refine workflows and increase productivity impact over time. jobs.description: Who are we? Cohere is the leading security-first enterprise AI company. We build cutting-edge foundation AI models and end-to-end products that are designed to solve real-world business problems. We’re training and deploying frontier models for enterprises who are building AI systems. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI and we are looking for folks that want to be part of that. We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are all passionate about their craft. We are a global technology company co-headquartered in Toronto and San Francisco, with key offices in London, New York City, Montreal, Seoul, Germany and Paris. Join us! Why this role? To have the opportunity to collaborate with Cohere researchers and tools on designing and implementing novel research ideas and shipping state-of-the-art models to production. We have openings in teams covering base model training, retrieval augmented generation, data and evaluation, safety, and finetuning, to name a few; and we are open to receiving intern applications in any research area relating to LLMs to broaden your research connections while obtaining deep experience in a growing AI startup.  Please Note: To be eligible for a Research Internship, you must be currently pursuing a PhD in Machine Learning, NLP, or a related discipline. You need to be available for a full-time internship that lasts for 4-6 months. As a Cohere Research Intern, you will: Conduct cutting-edge machine learning research, building and training large language models.  Focus on research projects aimed at expanding the frontier of knowledge in language modelling and associate areas such as evaluation, multimodal models, optimisation etc. Disseminate your research results through the production of publications, datasets, and code. Contribute to research initiatives that have practical applications in Cohere’s product development.  You may be a good fit if you: Are currently pursuing, or in the process of obtaining, a PhD in Machine Learning, NLP, Artificial Intelligence, or a related discipline. We will also consider exceptional non-PhD candidates. Are eligible for work authorization in the country of employment at the time of hire and maintain ongoing work authorization throughout the internship period.  Have experience using large-scale distributed training strategies, data annotation and evaluation pipelines, or implementing state of the art ML models. Are familiar with autoregressive sequence models, such as Transformers. Have strong communication and problem-solving skills with the ability to convey complex research findings clearly and succinctly.  Have knowledge, or are knowledgeable, of programming languages such as Python, C, C++, Lua, or related languages. Have knowledge of related ML frameworks such as JAX, Pytorch and Tensorflow. Have previous experience in building systems based on machine learning and deep learning techniques.  Demonstrate passion for applied NLP models and products. Preferred Qualifications: Demonstrated expertise through publications in top tier venues in fields such as machine learning, NLP, artificial intelligence, computer vision, optimization, computer science, statistics, applied mathematics, or data science.  Proven ability to tackle analytical problems using quantitative methodologies.  Proficiency in handling and analysing complex, high-dimensional data from various sources. Experience in applying theoretical and empirical research to real-world problem-solving. How and Where We Work: Cohere is remote-friendly. We have offices in Toronto, San Francisco, New York City, London, Paris, Montreal, and more coming soon. We build cutting-edge foundation AI models and end-to-end products that are designed to solve real-world business problems. Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are all passionate about their craft. We are a global technology company co-headquartered in Toronto and San Francisco, with key offices in London, New York City, Montreal, Seoul, Germany and Paris. Join us! Why this role? Ship state of the art models to production. Design and implement novel research ideas. Build elegant training/deployment pipelines. Join us at a pivotal moment, shape what we build and wear multiple hats as an intern! Our recruitment process will begin in the upcoming weeks, and we will be carefully reviewing applications and assessing potential candidates for our internships. Should we find a suitable match with your qualifications and our requirements, we will be in touch to discuss the opportunity further and to advance your application to the next stage Please Note: To be eligible for this position you should be a student currently enrolled in a post-secondary program, available for a full-time 3-6 month internship, co-op, or research work term. As a Machine Learning Intern, you will: Design, train and improve upon cutting-edge models. Help us develop new techniques to train and serve models safer, better, and faster. Train extremely large-scale models on massive datasets. Explore continual and active learning strategies for streaming data. Learn from experienced senior machine learning technical staff. Work closely with product teams to develop solutions. You may be a good fit if you have: Proficiency in Python and related ML frameworks such as Tensorflow, TF-Serving, JAX, and XLA/MLIR. Experience using large-scale distributed training strategies. Familiarity with autoregressive sequence models, such as Transformers. Strong communication and problem-solving skills. A demonstrated passion for applied NLP models and products. Bonus : experience writing kernels for GPUs using CUDA. Bonus : experience training on TPUs. Bonus : papers at top-tier venues (such as NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AIStats, MLSys, JMLR, AAAI, Nature, COLING, ACL, EMNLP). How and Where We Work: Cohere is remote-friendly. For those not near an office: a co-working benefit so you can work alongside others in your city. If any of the above doesn’t line up exactly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply. We strive to create an inclusive work environment for all; we welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form , and we will work together to meet your needs. We may use AI-enabled tools to screen and assess applicants against the criteria for this position. This helps our recruiters identify potentially qualified candidates, but it doesn't limit the applications our recruiters may review or consider.

jobs.description:
At Retired.com, we’re on a mission to give everyday Americans control over their financial future. As the parent company of BitcoinIRA, Rocket Dollar, and Digital Trust, we sit at the intersection of fintech innovation and retirement planning — empowering clients to invest in alternative assets like cryptocurrency, real estate, and private equity through self-directed IRAs and other tax-advantaged accounts. We’re a fast-moving, fully remote team that’s building the financial infrastructure for the next generation of retirement investing. If you want your work to matter and you thrive in an environment where you can make a real impact, you’ll fit right in here.
The Opportunity
The Business Systems Analyst serves as the Technology organization’s internal consultant to Finance, Marketing, Sales, and Operations — advising business unit leaders on technology capabilities and constraints, translating operational strategy into actionable system requirements, and ensuring that technology investments align with business performance objectives. This role leads the end-to-end requirements lifecycle: from advising senior leadership on what is technically feasible and strategically sound, to developing the functional specifications that engineering executes against. The BSA exercises significant professional judgment in evaluating requirements scope and quality, manages competing project priorities across multiple business units, and is directly accountable for the accuracy and completeness of requirements entering the engineering pipeline.
\n What you'll do:

Serve as the Technology organization’s internal consultant to Finance, Marketing, Sales, and Operations — advising business unit leaders on technology capabilities, system constraints, and feasibility trade-offs that affect operational strategy and technology investment decisions.
Lead the development of functional and non-functional requirements, user stories, process flows, and acceptance criteria. Evaluate conflicting stakeholder input and make recommendations on requirements scope — including recommending de-scoping of items that do not meet feasibility, priority, or business-value thresholds — and drive resolution in collaboration with stakeholders and project leadership.
Develop, maintain, and enforce the Technology organization’s requirements management standards, documentation templates, and quality review protocols; assess requirements readiness and recommend when they meet the threshold for engineering handoff.
Serve as the lead facilitator for resolving requirements disputes between business units; surface conflicting priorities, present trade-off analyses, and drive alignment — escalating to senior leadership for final resolution when stakeholders cannot reach consensus.
Provide expert recommendations to the VP of Project Management and senior business leadership on technology roadmap sequencing, build-vs-configure-vs-buy trade-offs, and resource allocation decisions affecting the technology portfolio, supporting informed decision-making by leadership and cross-functional teams.
Analyze existing systems and workflows to identify, prioritize, and recommend solutions for gaps and inefficiencies; propose investigation scope and remediation approaches to technology leadership for approval within established project parameters.
Collaborate with data and analytics teams to define the reporting, metrics, and insights needed to measure business outcomes; recommend measurement frameworks and KPI definitions for stakeholder approval.
Maintain current, traceable documentation across all active projects; accountable for requirements quality such that defects traceable to requirements gaps are a direct performance measure of this role.
Leverage AI tools to accelerate requirements development, flowchart creation, acceptance criteria authorship, and documentation — applying professional judgment to validate and refine AI-generated outputs before engineering handoff.

What we're looking

jobs.description: Job Description At EisnerAmper, we look for individuals who welcome new ideas, encourage innovation, and are eager to make an impact. The Performance Associate is responsible for supporting the calculation, analysis, and reporting of investment performance for clients across the CI Wealth lines of business. This role ensures accurate and timely performance data, provides insights to support client reporting, and collaborates with internal teams to enhance performance measurement processes. What You Will Do: Performance Calculation: Validate reasonability of portfolio performance calculations, including time-weighted and money-weighted returns, attribution analysis, and risk-adjusted measures. Validate data inputs and outputs to ensure accuracy and compliance with industry standards. Reconcile performance results with custodians, third-party providers, and internal systems. Reporting & Analysis: Prepare and distribute performance reports for clients, advisors, and management, ensuring clarity and alignment with organizational objectives. Conduct detailed performance attribution and contribution analysis to identify drivers of portfolio performance. Support ad-hoc analysis requests to provide insights for client reviews, business development, or regulatory reporting. Process Improvement: Collaborate with Technology and other Operations teams to enhance performance calculation methodologies and streamline workflows. Identify and implement automation opportunities to improve efficiency and reduce errors. Provide recommendations for updates to performance databases, ensuring data integrity and consistency. Compliance & Standards: Ensure performance calculations and reporting adhere to regulatory requirements and internal policies. Stakeholder Collaboration: Work closely with portfolio managers, advisors, Client Experience teams, Technology, and other Shared Services teams to address performance-related inquiries. jobs.description: Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on LinkedIn, X, and Instagram. Blackstone Private Wealth was established to answer the growing demand for Blackstone products from high-net worth investors. Partnering with many of the world’s largest private banks and wealth management firms as well as family offices, Blackstone’s Private Wealth team packages and delivers the full breadth of Blackstone’s alternative product capability to these firms and their clients as well as providing ongoing education, training, and support around alternatives. Specific responsibilities include: Field operational inquiries from Financial Advisors & internal sales teams on Blackstone investment offerings. Liaise with Blackstone business units, transfer agents, and other third-party service providers relating to client inquiries, problem resolutions, and reconciliations. Project management across various groups within Blackstone Private Wealth to enhance collaboration and efficiencies. Identify and document operational procedures for ongoing fund maintenance on various platforms. Develop strong understanding of distribution partners’ operational platforms and processes and become resident Blackstone expert for advisors. Assist Financial Advisors with problem resolution of account opening, maintenance items and other inquiries related to client onboarding, servicing and client retention (deadlines, platform questions, redemptions, capital calls, distributions). Assist in ad hoc projects / research to support Blackstone Private Wealth business initiatives. Qualifications: Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment, and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. The Investor Services Associate should exhibit strong technical and analytical skills. Candidates should be driven, detailed and team-oriented with innovative ideas and solutions who thrive on challenges in a dynamic environment. In addition, the successful candidate must meet the following qualifications: Bachelor’s Degree 1+ years of financial industry experience Strong verbal and written communication skills Attention to detail and accuracy Strong problem analysis and resolution skills Identify and summarize issues and recommendations Work efficiently in a deadline driven environment Manage multiple projects simultaneously Work independently in a team‐oriented environment Adaptable and collaborative Strong understanding of the high net worth and Financial Advisor operating model Knowledge of alternative investment funds and operational terms is helpful Develop strong working relationships with colleagues, teams, and clients The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position.
